title: Story dependencies with directed edges and cycle detection

story.i_want_to: create and remove directed dependency edges between stories (story A depends_on story B), list story dependencies within an epic, and have the system reject cycles
story.so_that: the orchestrator can determine which stories must be verified before others can be assigned, ensuring work proceeds in the correct order

rationale: Story dependencies are finer-grained than epic dependencies and control the actual work sequencing. A story can only be assigned to an agent once all its dependency stories have been verified. This ensures implementation builds on verified foundations rather than unverified claims. The cycle detection reuses the same graph algorithm as epic dependencies.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.WorkBreakdown.StoryDependency schema exists with fields: id, tenant_id, story_id (the dependent), depends_on_story_id (the prerequisite), inserted_at
POST /api/v1/story_dependencies (role: user) creates a dependency edge; requires story_id and depends_on_story_id
Creating a dependency that would form a cycle returns 422 with error message 'Cycle detected in dependency graph'
Creating a duplicate dependency returns 409 Conflict
Story dependencies can cross epic boundaries within the same project (story in epic 2 depends on story in epic 1)
Both stories must belong to the same project (determined via their parent epics); cross-project dependencies are rejected
DELETE /api/v1/story_dependencies/:id (role: user) removes a dependency edge
GET /api/v1/epics/:id/story_dependencies (role: agent+) returns all story dependency edges for stories within an epic (and cross-epic deps involving those stories)
All operations are scoped to the current tenant via RLS and application-level checks. RLS is enabled on the story_dependencies table.
Every mutation (create, update, delete) MUST be recorded in the audit log via Ecto.Multi in the same transaction as the entity change.

Story dependency creation MUST validate cross-level consistency: if the dependent story is in Epic A and the prerequisite story is in Epic B, and Epic B depends on Epic A (via epic_dependencies), this creates a cross-level deadlock and MUST return 422 with a clear error message.

test_cases.name: Create valid story dependency within same epic
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id, number: "1.1"}) as story_a
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id, number: "1.2"}) as story_b
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}
expected_results: Dependency exists in database with correct tenant_id

test_cases.name: Create cross-epic story dependency within same project
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, number: 1}) as epic_1
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, number: 2}) as epic_2
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_1.id, number: "1.1"}) as story_1_1
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_2.id, number: "2.1"}) as story_2_1
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}

test_cases.name: Reject cycle in story dependencies
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story_a
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story_b
fixture(:story_dependency, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, story_id: story_b.id, depends_on_story_id: story_a.id})
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}
expected_results: Error message contains 'Cycle detected in dependency graph'

test_cases.name: Reject self-dependency
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}
expected_results: Error message indicates self-dependency is not allowed

test_cases.name: Reject cross-project story dependency
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project_a
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project_b
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project_a.id}) as epic_a
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project_b.id}) as epic_b
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_a.id}) as story_a
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_b.id}) as story_b
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}
expected_results: Error message indicates stories must belong to the same project

test_cases.name: Delete story dependency
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story_a
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story_b
fixture(:story_dependency, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, story_id: story_b.id, depends_on_story_id: story_a.id}) as dep
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}
expected_results: Dependency no longer exists in database

test_cases.name: List story dependencies for epic includes cross-epic deps
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, number: 1}) as epic_1
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, number: 2}) as epic_2
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_1.id}) as story_1
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_2.id}) as story_2
fixture(:story_dependency, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, story_id: story_2.id, depends_on_story_id: story_1.id})
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :agent}) returns {raw_key, _}
expected_results: Response includes the cross-epic dependency (story_2 depends on story_1)

test_cases.name: Mutation creates audit log entries for story dependency operations
preconditions: tenant = fixture(:tenant)
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story_a
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story_b
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}
steps: POST /api/v1/story_dependencies with body {"story_id": "<story_b.id>", "depends_on_story_id": "<story_a.id>"} and user API key
expected_results: Audit log entry created with entity_type='story_dependency', entity_id=new_dep.id, action='created', actor_type='api_key', actor_id=api_key.id
Audit log entry created with entity_type='story_dependency', entity_id=dep.id, action='deleted', actor_type='api_key', actor_id=api_key.id
All audit entries are in the same transaction as the entity changes (no orphaned entity without audit)

test_cases.name: Cross-level deadlock: story dependency conflicts with epic dependency
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic_a
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic_b
fixture(:epic_dependency, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_a.id, depends_on_epic_id: epic_b.id})
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_a.id, number: "1.1"}) as story_s1
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic_b.id, number: "2.1"}) as story_s2
fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :user}) returns {raw_key, _}
expected_results: Error message identifies the cross-level deadlock: Epic A depends on Epic B but story S2 (in Epic B) would depend on story S1 (in Epic A)

test_cases.name: Cross-tenant isolation: tenant A cannot access tenant B data
preconditions: tenant_a = fixture(:tenant)
tenant_b = fixture(:tenant)
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id}) as project_a
fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, project_id: project_a.id}) as epic_a
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, epic_id: epic_a.id}) as story_a
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, epic_id: epic_a.id}) as story_b
fixture(:story_dependency, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, story_id: story_b.id, depends_on_story_id: story_a.id})
expected_results: Returns empty list or 404, never tenant_a's data

technical_notes: Reuse the same cycle detection algorithm from US-6.3 (Loopctl.WorkBreakdown.Graph module)
To check same-project constraint: join story -> epic -> project for both stories and compare project_ids
Cross-epic dependencies are explicitly allowed — a story in Epic 3 can depend on a story in Epic 1
When a story is deleted (US-6.2), cascade delete its dependency edges (both directions)
The list endpoint for an epic should return deps where either story_id or depends_on_story_id belongs to a story in that epic
Schema module MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` (provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`)
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
